issues.title: AI Copilot uses no real project data — all insights are placeholder or empty
issues.description: getAiInsights falls back to empty arrays or onboarding placeholders when Supabase has no ai_insights rows and the AI service is unconfigured. The copilot never queries live RFIs, budget, schedule, or punch list data to synthesize real insights, making the entire intelligence module feel like a demo.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/ai.ts, in the getAiInsights function: BEFORE: When cachedInsights and budgetInsights are both empty, the function falls through to a comment about querying real project data but never actually does so — it returns empty or placeholder onboarding insights. AFTER: Before returning the empty/onboarding fallback, query real project data to generate dynamic insights. Add these queries after the `const now` declaration: (1) Query `rfis` table for count where status='open' and due_date < now, grouped to get overdue count. (2) Query `punch_list_items` for open count. (3) Query `budget_line_items` for any where `spent_to_date > current_budget`. (4) Query `schedule_activities` for any where status='in_progress' and planned_finish < now. For each query that returns results > 0, push a constructed AIInsight object into a `dynamicInsights` array with realistic titles like 'X RFIs are overdue and may delay critical path', severity based on count thresholds (>5 critical, >2 warning, else info), type 'risk' or 'action_needed', confidence 0.85, source 'computed'. Return `{ insights: dynamicInsights, dataSource: 'computed' }` if dynamicInsights.length > 0 instead of the onboarding fallback. Keep the onboarding fallback only if ALL queries return zero results. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: CopilotPanel chat messages lack ARIA live region and keyboard navigation
issues.description: The chat message list has no aria-live attribute so screen readers never announce new AI responses. The panel has no role='dialog' or aria-label. Message input textarea lacks an accessible label. Keyboard users cannot close the panel with Escape.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: BEFORE: The outer panel div has no ARIA attributes. The messages scroll container has no aria-live. The AgentMentionInput textarea relies on a placeholder but has no explicit aria-label. There is no onKeyDown handler for Escape to close. AFTER: (1) Add role='dialog', aria-label='AI Copilot', and aria-modal='true' to the outermost motion.aside or panel container div. (2) Add aria-live='polite' and aria-relevant='additions' to the messages scroll container div. (3) Pass textareaAriaLabel='Type a message for AI Copilot' prop to AgentMentionInput (it already accepts this prop). (4) Add a useEffect that listens for keydown 'Escape' on document and calls the close/toggle function from useCopilotStore when the panel is open. Clean up the listener on unmount. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: FloatingAIButton overlaps bottom navigation on mobile and panel is not optimized for field use
issues.description: The floating button uses a fixed bottom position that may overlap mobile bottom nav bars. The button size at 56px on mobile is acceptable but the safe area calculation does not account for all devices. There is no haptic feedback on tap. The button should be larger (64px) on mobile for muddy-glove use.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, in the FloatingAIButton component: BEFORE: Mobile button width/height is '56px' with bottom position 'calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px) + 72px)'. The button has no touch-action CSS and no specific mobile touch optimizations. AFTER: (1) Change mobile button width and height from '56px' to '64px' to meet the 44px minimum Apple HIG with margin for gloved hands. (2) Change the bottom position calculation to 'calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px) + 80px)' to add more clearance above bottom navigation. (3) Add touchAction: 'manipulation' to the button style to prevent double-tap zoom on mobile. (4) Add the CSS property WebkitTapHighlightColor: 'transparent' to eliminate the default tap highlight. (5) Increase the Sparkles icon size inside the button from the current value to 28 on mobile (add a ternary using the existing isMobile state). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: AgentMentionInput silently drops messages on send failure with no retry or error feedback
issues.description: The handleSend function calls onSend(value) but wraps it in no try/catch. If the parent's onSend throws (network error, rate limit), the input clears the text but the user sees no error. The message appears lost. No toast notification, no retry mechanism, no visual error state.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx, in the AgentMentionInput component: BEFORE: The handleSend function sets isSending true, calls onSend(trimmed), then clears value and sets isSending false — all without try/catch. If onSend is async and throws, isSending stays true and the message text is lost. AFTER: (1) Wrap the onSend call in a try/catch block. (2) In the try block, await onSend(trimmed) — add async to the handleSend callback if not already. (3) Only call setValue('') inside the try block after successful send. (4) In the catch block, call toast.error('Message failed to send. Please try again.') using the already-imported toast from 'sonner'. Do NOT clear the input value on error so the user can retry. (5) Always set isSending(false) in a finally block. (6) Remove the existing setIsSending(false) that comes after onSend since it will now be in finally. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: AI conversation history not persisted to Supabase — conversations lost on page refresh
issues.description: The CopilotPanel uses useMultiAgentChat which stores messages in local React state only. When the panel closes or the page refreshes, the entire conversation history is lost. The ai_conversations and ai_messages tables exist in the schema but are never written to. Enterprise GCs need audit trails of AI interactions for compliance and to review AI recommendations that led to project decisions.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: BEFORE: Messages are rendered from the useMultiAgentChat hook's local state. There is no call to supabase to persist conversations. The supabase import exists at the top of the file but is only used for real-time channel subscriptions. AFTER: (1) After the existing useEffect hooks, add a new useEffect that fires when the messages array changes and has length > 0. Inside it, define an async function persistConversation that: (a) checks if a conversation_id exists in local state (add a conversationIdRef = useRef<string|null>(null) at component top), (b) if no conversation_id, inserts a new row into ai_conversations with project_id from useCopilotStore or a projectId prop, user_id from supabase.auth.getUser(), conversation_topic 'general', and stores the returned id in conversationIdRef, (c) takes the last message from the array and inserts it into ai_messages with conversation_id, role, content, and metadata containing a timestamp. (2) Wrap the supabase calls in try/catch — on error, log to console.error but do NOT block the UI. (3) Add a cleanup comment noting this is best-effort persistence. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

invented_features.title: Project Pulse Morning Briefing
invented_features.description: Auto-generated one paragraph natural language summary of project health sent to PM and superintendent at 6 AM every morning. Pulls from overnight weather changes, overdue items that crossed thresholds, schedule activities starting today, and any safety certifications expiring this week. Delivered as both an in-app notification card on the Dashboard and an email digest. No competitor has this — Procore Assist answers questions but never proactively briefs the team.

competitive_intel: Procore's AI Copilot (Procore Assist) can search specs, RFIs, and submittals conversationally, and their new Agent Builder lets users create no-code AI agents. SiteSync's multi-agent architecture with specialist agents (schedule, cost, safety, quality, compliance, document) and generative UI blocks is architecturally more sophisticated than Procore's single-agent approach. However, Procore's Assist is actually connected to real project data at scale, while SiteSync's copilot currently returns placeholder insights when the AI service is unconfigured. The generative UI system (charts, approval cards, cost breakdowns, safety alerts, RFI responses, photo grids) is a genuine differentiator that no competitor ships — but it needs real data flowing through it to matter. Autodesk Construction IQ offers AI risk prediction from project data but has no conversational interface. The gap is closing: SiteSync must wire real Supabase data into the AI pipeline this cycle to maintain its architectural advantage.
summary: The Project Intelligence module has an impressive multi-agent architecture with 13 generative UI component types, specialist agent routing via @mentions, batch action previews, and a rich copilot panel — architecturally ahead of any competitor. However, the module's Achilles heel is that almost none of this connects to real project data. The AI insights endpoint falls back to empty arrays or onboarding placeholders because neither the AI service nor the Supabase ai_insights table has real data. Conversations are not persisted to the database, violating enterprise audit requirements. Accessibility is poor: the copilot panel lacks ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support. The floating AI button needs mobile optimization for field crews. Error handling in the chat input silently drops failed messages. The five issues target: (1) wiring real project queries into the insights pipeline, (2) adding ARIA and keyboard support to the copilot, (3) optimizing the mobile touch target, (4) adding error recovery to message sending, and (5) persisting conversations to Supabase for enterprise compliance.
